Nano Express 5 (2024) 035008
O Eyilcim et al
Compound 4a-b (0.25 mmol, 1.0 equiv), sodium carbonate (0.50 mmol, 2.0 equiv), compound 2 (0.30 mmol,
1.2 equiv), and hexaﬂuoro isopropanol (HFIP, 1.0 ml) were added sequentially to an oven-dried Schlenk tube.
After the tube was closed, the reaction mixture was allowed to stir at room temperature for 12–18 h. After
ﬁnishing the starting materials, it was diluted with ethyl acetate, ﬁltered using celite and washed with ethyl
acetate. The solvent was evaporated under reduced pressure and the resulting residue was puriﬁed by column
0.28 mmol of Compound 4a-b was weighed into a single-necked ﬂask. 0.6 mmol phenyl acetylene, 0.006 mmol
CuSO4.5H2O, 0.012 mmol sodium ascorbate, 0.06 mmol benzoic acid and 1:2 (0.2:0.4 ml) t-BuOH:H2O were
added. The reaction was stirred at room temperature for 18 h. As a result of TLC control, the reaction was
terminated, and extraction was performed with dichloromethane. It was dried with Na2SO4, the solvent was
removed under low pressure, and the crude product was puriﬁed with the help of column chromatography.
